title: Aureline install-topology truth-surface row
description: Boundary schema for one install_topology_truth_row_record. Each row binds one supported install-profile id (drawn from artifacts/release/install_topology_matrix.yaml) to (1) the install-mode / channel / updater-owner / binary-root / side-by-side-relation classes the row exposes as customer-visible truth, (2) the file-association and protocol-handler ownership classes the row publishes on Help/About and diagnostics, (3) the revert-path class the row publishes (so the row cannot quietly imply unsupported recovery), (4) the silent-deployment baseline class the row claims (so M1 cannot claim enterprise depth the product has not built), (5) the durable state-root class refs (read into artifacts/release/state_root_map.yaml), (6) the M1 truth-surface families that expose the row, and (7) a named failure drill that the validation lane reproduces. This schema is a truth-surface contract, not a runtime evaluator; it does not implement an installer, updater, or fleet tool.

install_topology_truth_row_schema_versio.description: Integer schema version for install-topology truth rows. Current value is 1.

install_truth_profile_id.description: Stable, machine-readable id for the install-truth profile row (for example per_user_installed.stable, portable.portable_stable). Lowercase dot/underscore/hyphen segments only.

binary_root_class.description: Binary-root class the row publishes on Help/About and diagnostics.

side_by_side_relation_class.description: Side-by-side relation class. side_by_side_preview rows MUST NOT publish 'none' (they must declare which paired channel they sit beside).

file_association_ownership_class.description: File-association registration ownership class published on Help/About. Mirrors state_root_map.yaml file_association_registration_class.

protocol_handler_ownership_class.description: Protocol/URI scheme handler ownership class published on Help/About. Mirrors state_root_map.yaml protocol_handler_ownership_class.

revert_path_class.description: Revert / rollback path class the row publishes. managed_deployed rows MUST NOT publish 'unsupported' (they must name a managed_pin_to_prior_build or package_manager_revert path).

silent_deployment_baseline_class.description: Silent-deployment baseline class the row claims. M1 baseline forbids 'managed_silent_full' (enterprise depth the product has not yet executed); the absence of that enum value is intentional.

m1_truth_surface_class.description: M1 truth-surface family that exposes the row. help_about MUST appear on every row so users can always inspect install topology from the About pane.

durable_state_root_class_ref.description: Opaque ref to a durable_state_root_class value in artifacts/release/state_root_map.yaml. Consumers treat this as opaque text and do not parse it.

drill_id.description: Stable id for the row's named failure drill, drawn from the envelope's failure_drill_id_vocabulary.

forced_input.description: Forced row mutation applied under --force-drill so the runner can reproduce the named expected_check_id from a clean tree.

expected_check_id.description: Exact check_id the runner must report under --force-drill.

actionable_next_action.description: Short reviewer-readable next action when the drill fires unexpectedly.

failure_drill.description: Named failure drill the validation lane reproduces under --force-drill. Required on every row so omissions stay loud.

durable_state_root_class_refs.description: Durable state-root class refs the row owns. Empty is non-conforming; even portable rows declare a portable_colocated_root.

paired_channel_class.description: Required non-null channel reference when side_by_side_relation_class is not 'none'. Names the paired channel the row sits beside; the lane forbids 'none' relation on side_by_side_preview rows and forbids non-null paired_channel_class when relation is 'none'.

m1_truth_surfaces.description: M1 truth-surface families that expose the row. MUST include 'help_about'.

owner_dri.description: Named owner DRI for the install-truth profile row.

install_profile_card_ref.description: Opaque ref into artifacts/release/install_topology_matrix.yaml (for example install_profile_card.<id>). The lane resolves the upstream matrix exists and does not parse the ref further.

notes.description: Optional short reviewer-facing note about the row.

allOf.description: side_by_side_preview rows must publish a non-'none' side_by_side_relation_class and a non-null paired_channel_class.

Rows whose side_by_side_relation_class is one of the non-paired classes MUST set paired_channel_class to null. The '_isolated' classes are explicit no-pair relations for portable and managed-image rows.
managed_deployed rows MUST NOT publish 'unsupported' as the revert path.
portable rows MUST publish portable_swap as the revert path and portable_swap_no_silent_required as the silent-deployment baseline.
